name: Raspberry Pi 3 Model B+
description: Older but still in production; cheap entry point

status.comment: Raspberry Pi Ltd guarantees production until at least January 2030 (per official product page, checked 2026-04-26).

architecture-family.comment: BCM2837B0 is ARMv8-A 64-bit; Raspberry Pi OS historically defaulted to 32-bit but a 64-bit image is officially supported.

open-source-hardware.comment: Only a reduced schematic PDF is publicly available; full schematics and PCB design files are not released under an OSHW-compatible license.

country-of-origin.comment: Designed by Raspberry Pi Ltd / Raspberry Pi Foundation in Cambridge, UK.

process-node.comment: BCM2837/BCM2837B0 fabricated on TSMC 40nm. Broadcom does not publish this directly; widely reported by independent press.

gpu.comment: Dual-core VideoCore IV @ 400 MHz core / 300 MHz V3D.

vulkan-support.comment: Official Vulkan effort (Mesa v3dv) targets Raspberry Pi 4 and later (VideoCore VI). VideoCore IV on Pi 3 has only an experimental, non-conformant community driver (rpi-vk-driver).

hw-video-decode.comment: 1080p60 H.264/MPEG-4 AVC high-profile decode in hardware. MPEG-2 and VC-1 require paid licenses; H.265/VP9/AV1 are not hardware-decoded on VideoCore IV.

hw-video-encode.comment: 1080p30 H.264 encode in hardware via VideoCore IV.

ram-max-gb.comment: Only a 1 GB configuration is sold.

ram-type.comment: Vendor product page lists '1GB LPDDR2 SDRAM'; tag set has no LPDDR2 option, so LPDDR3 is used as the closest available tag. Actual technology is LPDDR2.

ethernet.comment: Gigabit Ethernet PHY routed over USB 2.0; real-world maximum throughput ~300 Mbps.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Power input is micro-USB, not USB-C.

poe-support.comment: PoE-capable header on board, but PoE itself requires the separately-sold Raspberry Pi PoE HAT.

max-display-resolution.comment: VideoCore IV HDMI output is officially 1920×1200 at 60 Hz; 1080p60 is the typical advertised maximum.

audio-jack.comment: 4-pole 3.5 mm jack carrying analog stereo audio plus composite video.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Reference 40-pin RPi GPIO header — this board defines the pinout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Standard HAT-compliant: header position, mounting holes, and EEPROM auto-config.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, and UART all available on the standard 40-pin GPIO header.

idle-power-w.comment: ~459 mA × 5 V ≈ 2.3 W idle with monitor, keyboard, mouse and Wi-Fi connected (Wikipedia, citing Raspberry Pi Foundation measurements).

load-power-w.comment: ~1.13 A × 5 V ≈ 5.7 W under stress (Wikipedia, citing Raspberry Pi Foundation measurements).

cooling-required.comment: BCM2837B0 ships with an integrated metal heat-spreader; vendor does not require a heatsink for sustained loads, though heatsink/case airflow helps prevent throttling.

form-factor-class.comment: Defines the Pi-standard form factor (~85.6 × 56.5 mm).

weight-g.comment: Adafruit lists 49.7 g (1.8 oz) bare board weight.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: BCM2837 is supported in mainline Linux (arch/arm64/boot/dts/broadcom/bcm2837-rpi-3-b-plus.dts). Raspberry Pi maintains its own fork with extra drivers, but a stock upstream kernel boots with all major peripherals working.

distros-with-images.comment: Pi 3 B+ has board-specific images from a wide range of distros, including the official Raspberry Pi OS in 32- and 64-bit flavours.

bootloader.comment: Boot is initiated by the Broadcom VideoCore GPU running closed-source firmware (bootcode.bin / start.elf); U-Boot can be chained but is not the primary bootloader.

long-term-support.comment: Raspberry Pi Ltd guarantees production until at least January 2030, and Raspberry Pi OS continues to ship 32- and 64-bit images for this board.

android-support.comment: No officially maintained Android image from Raspberry Pi Ltd. Community LineageOS / Android-IoT ports exist but are unofficial and intermittently maintained.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch MSRP $35 (March 2018); still the suggested retail at major resellers as of 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: Widely in stock at major resellers as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive official documentation portal, mechanical drawings, reduced schematics, and an active official magazine. PCB design files are not published, but for end-user docs this is best-in-class.

community-size.comment: Raspberry Pi has the largest hobbyist SBC community by a wide margin.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Vast first-party and third-party ecosystem: cases, displays, cameras, HATs, PoE HAT, official PSU, etc.
